Package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils

Class Shorts

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts
Direct Known Subclasses:
BasicTestUtils
public class Shorts extends Object
Terse alias facade for the domain utility classes.

A single wildcard static import (import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;) exposes the entire short-name vocabulary. Almost every method here is a one-line delegation to a canonical, fully-descriptive method on a domain class — behavior is identical to calling the canonical method directly — and each such alias documents its target via an @see tag. Canonical implementations live in ObjectUtils, StringUtils, CollectionUtils, ClassUtils, ThrowableUtils, IoUtils, FileUtils, AssertionUtils, PredicateUtils, SystemUtils, DateUtils, and ReflectionUtils.

Documented exception: the terse exception factories (rex/brex/ iaex/isex/uoex/uoroex/ioex/exex) are self-contained — they construct the exception directly rather than delegating to a domain class — so that the domain *Utils classes can stay Shorts-free (they use the package-private Exceptions helper instead). These are the only methods in this class with no @see tag.

Disjointness invariant: no Shorts alias name is identical to the canonical (full) method name of any method in any domain class. This lets a developer wildcard-import Shorts.* alongside any single domain *Utils.* with zero method-name collisions.

This class is the replacement for the former Utils grab-bag (now deleted): callers replace import static ...utils.Utils.* with import static ...utils.Shorts.*.

    • nn

      public static boolean nn(Object o)
      Returns true if the specified object is not null.
      Parameters:
      o - The object to check.
      Returns:
      true if the object is not null.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.isNotNull(Object)

      public static <T> boolean n(T v)
      Returns true if the specified value is null.

      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      v - The value to check.
      Returns:
      true if the value is null.

      public static boolean n(Object... v)
      Returns true if the specified array is null or any of its elements are null.
      Parameters:
      v - The values to check.
      Returns:
      true if the array is null or contains a null element.

      • ObjectUtils.isNull(Object...)

    • eq

      public static <T> boolean eq(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the two objects are equal (null-safe).
      Type Parameters:
      T - The object type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.equal(Object,Object)

    • eq

      public static boolean eq(boolean ci, String a, String b)
      Returns true if the two strings are equal, optionally ignoring case.
      Parameters:
      ci - If true, comparison is case-insensitive.
      a - The first string.
      b - The second string.
      Returns:
      true if the strings are equal.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.equal(boolean,String,String)

    • eq

      public static <T, U> boolean eq(T a, U b, BiPredicate<T,U> t)
      Returns true if the two objects are equal using the specified equality test.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The first object type.
      U - The second object type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      t - The equality predicate.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal per the predicate.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.equal(Object,Object,BiPredicate)

    • eqa

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> boolean eqa(T a, T... b)
      Returns true if the first object equals any of the remaining objects.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The object type.
      Parameters:
      a - The object to look for.
      b - The candidate values.
      Returns:
      true if a match is found.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.equalsAny(Object,Object...)

    • eqic

      public static boolean eqic(Object a, Object b)
      Returns true if the two objects are equal ignoring case (via their string forms).
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal ignoring case.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.equalIgnoreCase(Object,Object)

    • neq

      public static <T> boolean neq(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the two objects are not equal (null-safe).

      T - The object type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are not equal.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.notEqual(Object,Object)

    • neq

      public static <T, U> boolean neq(T a, U b, BiPredicate<T,U> t)
      Returns true if the two objects are not equal using the specified equality test.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The first object type.
      U - The second object type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      t - The equality predicate.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are not equal per the predicate.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.notEqual(Object,Object,BiPredicate)

    • cmp

      public static int cmp(Object a, Object b)
      Compares two objects for order (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      a - The first object.
      b - The second object.
      Returns:
      A negative, zero, or positive integer as a is less than, equal to, or greater than b.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.compare(Object,Object)

    • lt

      public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lt(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the first comparable is less than the second.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The comparable type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first value.
      b - The second value.
      Returns:
      true if a < b.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.lessThan(Comparable,Comparable)

    • lte

      public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lte(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the first comparable is less than or equal to the second.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The comparable type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first value.
      b - The second value.
      Returns:
      true if a <= b.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.lessThanOrEqual(Comparable,Comparable)

    • gt

      public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean gt(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the first comparable is greater than the second.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The comparable type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first value.
      b - The second value.
      Returns:
      true if a > b.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.greaterThan(Comparable,Comparable)

    • gte

      public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean gte(T a, T b)
      Returns true if the first comparable is greater than or equal to the second.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The comparable type.
      Parameters:
      a - The first value.
      b - The second value.
      Returns:
      true if a >= b.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.greaterThanOrEqual(Comparable,Comparable)

    • or

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> T or(T... t)
      Returns the first non-null argument, or null if all are null.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      t - The candidate values in priority order.
      Returns:
      The first non-null value, or null.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.coalesce(Object...)

    • or

      @SafeVarargs public static boolean or(boolean... v)
      Returns true if any of the specified booleans are true.
      Parameters:
      v - The booleans to check.
      Returns:
      true if at least one value is true.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.anyTrue(boolean...)

    • and

      @SafeVarargs public static boolean and(boolean... v)
      Returns true if all of the specified booleans are true.
      Parameters:
      v - The booleans to check.
      Returns:
      true if every value is true.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.allTrue(boolean...)

    • not

      public static boolean not(boolean v)
      Returns the logical negation of the specified boolean.
      Parameters:
      v - The value to negate.
      Returns:
      !v.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.negate(boolean)

    • b

      public static boolean b(Object v)
      Converts the specified object to a boolean.
      Parameters:
      v - The value to convert.
      Returns:
      The boolean value.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.toBoolean(Object)

    • s

      public static String s(Object v)
      Converts the specified object to its string representation (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      v - The value to stringify.
      Returns:
      The string form, or null if the value is null.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.stringify(Object)

    • id

      public static String id(Object o)
      Returns the system identity string of the specified object.
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The identity string (class name + identity hashcode).
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.identity(Object)

    • btw

      public static boolean btw(int n, int lo, int hi)
      Returns true if the specified integer is within the inclusive range.
      Parameters:
      n - The value to test.
      lo - The inclusive lower bound.
      hi - The inclusive upper bound.
      Returns:
      true if lo <= n <= hi.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.isBetween(int,int,int)

    • nm1

      public static <T extends Number> boolean nm1(T v)
      Returns true if the specified number is non-null and not equal to -1.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The number type.
      Parameters:
      v - The value to test.
      Returns:
      true if the value is not -1.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.isNotMinusOne(Number)

    • h

      public static int h(Object... v)
      Computes a hash code over the specified values.
      Parameters:
      v - The values to hash.
      Returns:
      The combined hash code.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.hash(Object...)

    • ie

      public static boolean ie(Object o)
      Returns true if the specified object is null or empty (string/collection/map/array).
      Parameters:
      o - The object to check.
      Returns:
      true if the object is null or empty.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.isEmpty(Object)

    • ine

      public static boolean ine(Object o)
      Returns true if the specified object is not null and not empty.
      Parameters:
      o - The object to check.
      Returns:
      true if the object is non-empty.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.isNotEmpty(Object)

    • o

      public static <T> Optional<T> o(T t)
      Wraps the specified value in an Optional.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      t - The value (may be null).
      Returns:
      An optional containing the value, or empty if null.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.optional(Object)

    • o

      public static <T> Optional<T> o(List<T> l, int i)
      Returns the element at the specified index of a list as an Optional.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      l - The list.
      i - The index.
      Returns:
      An optional containing the element, or empty if out of bounds.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.optionalAt(List,int)

    • oe

      public static <T> Optional<T> oe()
      Returns an empty Optional.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Returns:
      An empty optional.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.emptyOptional()

    • oo

      public static <T> T oo(Optional<T> o, T d)
      Returns the value of the optional, or the specified default if empty.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      o - The optional.
      d - The default value.
      Returns:
      The optional's value, or d if empty.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.orElse(Optional,Object)

    • rnn

      public static <T> T rnn(T v)
      Returns the specified value, throwing NullPointerException if it is null.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      v - The value.
      Returns:
      The non-null value.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.requireNonNull(Object)

    • sz

      public static int sz(Object o)
      Returns the size of the specified object (string/collection/map/array).
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The size, or 0 if null.
      See Also:
      • ObjectUtils.size(Object)

    • ie

      public static boolean ie(CharSequence s)
      Returns true if the specified character sequence is null or empty.
      Parameters:
      s - The sequence to check.
      Returns:
      true if null or zero-length.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.isEmpty(CharSequence)

    • ine

      public static boolean ine(CharSequence s)
      Returns true if the specified character sequence is not null and not empty.
      Parameters:
      s - The sequence to check.
      Returns:
      true if non-empty.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.isNotEmpty(CharSequence)

    • f

      public static String f(String p, Object... a)
      Formats a message using MessageFormat-style {0} placeholders.
      Parameters:
      p - The pattern.
      a - The arguments.
      Returns:
      The formatted string.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.format(String,Object...)

    • fs

      public static Supplier<String> fs(String p, Object... a)
      Returns a supplier that lazily formats a message (deferred until Supplier.get()).
      Parameters:
      p - The pattern.
      a - The arguments.
      Returns:
      A supplier of the formatted string.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.format(String,Object...)

    • r

      public static String r(Object o)
      Returns a human-readable string representation of the specified object.
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The readable representation.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.readable(Object)

    • ib

      public static boolean ib(String s)
      Returns true if the specified string is null, empty, or whitespace-only.
      Parameters:
      s - The string to check.
      Returns:
      true if blank.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.isBlank(CharSequence)

    • inb

      public static boolean inb(String s)
      Returns true if the specified string contains non-whitespace content.
      Parameters:
      s - The string to check.
      Returns:
      true if not blank.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.isNotBlank(CharSequence)

    • lc

      public static String lc(String s)
      Converts the specified string to lower case (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The lower-cased string, or null if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.lowerCase(String)

    • uc

      public static String uc(String s)
      Converts the specified string to upper case (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The upper-cased string, or null if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.upperCase(String)

    • tr

      public static String tr(String s)
      Trims leading/trailing whitespace from the specified string (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The trimmed string, or null if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.trim(String)

    • sw

      public static boolean sw(String s, String p)
      Returns true if the string starts with the specified prefix (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      p - The prefix.
      Returns:
      true if it starts with the prefix.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.startsWith(String,String)

    • ew

      public static boolean ew(String s, String x)
      Returns true if the string ends with the specified suffix (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      x - The suffix.
      Returns:
      true if it ends with the suffix.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.endsWith(String,String)

    • co

      public static boolean co(String s, String x)
      Returns true if the string contains the specified substring (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      x - The substring.
      Returns:
      true if it contains the substring.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.contains(String,String)

    • eqic

      public static boolean eqic(String a, String b)
      Returns true if the two strings are equal ignoring case (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      a - The first string.
      b - The second string.
      Returns:
      true if equal ignoring case.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase(String,String)

    • neqic

      public static boolean neqic(String a, String b)
      Returns true if the two strings are not equal ignoring case (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      a - The first string.
      b - The second string.
      Returns:
      true if not equal ignoring case.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.notEqualsIgnoreCase(String,String)

    • ein

      public static String ein(String s)
      Returns an empty string if the specified string is null, else the string.
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The string, or an empty string if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.emptyIfNull(String)

    • ein

      public static String ein(Object o)
      Returns an empty string if the specified object is null, else its string form.
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The string form, or an empty string if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.emptyIfNull(Object)

    • bin

      public static String bin(String s)
      Returns a blank (single-space) string if the specified string is null, else the string.
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The string, or a blank string if null.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.blankIfNull(String)

    • nie

      public static String nie(String s)
      Returns null if the specified string is empty, else the string.
      Parameters:
      s - The string.
      Returns:
      The string, or null if empty.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.nullIfEmpty(String)

    • sb

      public static StringBuilder sb(Object v)
      Creates a new StringBuilder initialized with the stringified form of the specified value.
      Parameters:
      v - The initial value. Can be null (renders as "null").
      Returns:
      A new string builder.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.newStringBuilder(Object)

    • sb

      public static StringBuilder sb(Object... v)
      Creates a new StringBuilder with the stringified form of each specified value appended in order.
      Parameters:
      v - The values to append in order (empty builder for no args).
      Returns:
      A new string builder.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.newStringBuilder(Object...)

    • cn

      public static String cn(Object o)
      Returns the fully-qualified class name of the specified object (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The class name, or null if the object is null.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.className(Object)

    • cns

      public static String cns(Object o)
      Returns the simple class name of the specified object (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The simple class name, or null if the object is null.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.classNameSimple(Object)

    • cnsq

      public static String cnsq(Object o)
      Returns the qualified simple class name of the specified object (includes enclosing classes).
      Parameters:
      o - The object.
      Returns:
      The qualified simple class name.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.classNameSimpleQualified(Object)

    • fne

      @SafeVarargs public static String fne(String... v)
      Returns the first non-empty string from the specified values.
      Parameters:
      v - The candidate strings.
      Returns:
      The first non-empty string, or null if all are empty.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.firstNonEmpty(String...)

    • fnb

      @SafeVarargs public static String fnb(String... v)
      Returns the first non-blank string from the specified values.
      Parameters:
      v - The candidate strings.
      Returns:
      The first non-blank string, or null if all are blank.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.firstNonBlank(String...)

    • jn

      @SafeVarargs public static String jn(String... v)
      Concatenates the specified strings.
      Parameters:
      v - The strings to join.
      Returns:
      The concatenated string.
      See Also:
      • StringUtils.join(String...)

    • a

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> T[] a(T... x)
      Creates an array from the specified elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      x - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.array(Object...)

    • l

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> List<T> l(T... x)
      Creates a modifiable ArrayList from the specified elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      x - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new modifiable list.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.list(Object...)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m()
      Creates an empty modifiable map.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Returns:
      An empty modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map()

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1)
      Creates a modifiable map with 1 entry.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - The key.
      v1 - The value.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2)
      Creates a modifiable map with 2 entries.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - The first key.
      v1 - The first value.
      k2 - The second key.
      v2 - The second value.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3)
      Creates a modifiable map with 3 entries.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - The first key.
      v1 - The first value.
      k2 - The second key.
      v2 - The second value.
      k3 - The third key.
      v3 - The third value.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4)
      Creates a modifiable map with 4 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2.
      k3 - Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5)
      Creates a modifiable map with 5 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3.
      k4 - Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6)
      Creates a modifiable map with 6 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3.
      k4 - Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7)
      Creates a modifiable map with 7 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
      k5 - Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8)
      Creates a modifiable map with 8 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4.
      k5 - Key 5. @param v5 Value 5. @param k6 Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9)
      Creates a modifiable map with 9 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
      k6 - Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8. @param k9 Key 9. @param v9 Value 9.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • m

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> m(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9, K k10, V v10)
      Creates a modifiable map with 10 entries (keys/values in alternating order).
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      k1 - Key 1. @param v1 Value 1. @param k2 Key 2. @param v2 Value 2. @param k3 Key 3. @param v3 Value 3. @param k4 Key 4. @param v4 Value 4. @param k5 Key 5. @param v5 Value 5.
      k6 - Key 6. @param v6 Value 6. @param k7 Key 7. @param v7 Value 7. @param k8 Key 8. @param v8 Value 8. @param k9 Key 9. @param v9 Value 9. @param k10 Key 10. @param v10 Value 10.
      Returns:
      A modifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.map(Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object,Object)

    • at

      public static <E> E at(List<E> l, int i)
      Returns the element at the specified index of a list (null-safe).
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      l - The list.
      i - The index.
      Returns:
      The element, or null if out of bounds.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.elementAt(List,int)

    • na

      public static <T> T[] na(Class<T> t)
      Creates an empty (zero-length) array of the specified component type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The component type.
      Parameters:
      t - The component class.
      Returns:
      An empty typed array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.nullArray(Class)

    • nie

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> nie(Map<K,V> v)
      Returns null if the specified map is empty, else the map.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      v - The map.
      Returns:
      The map, or null if empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(Map)

    • nie

      public static <E> List<E> nie(List<E> v)
      Returns null if the specified list is empty, else the list.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The list.
      Returns:
      The list, or null if empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(List)

    • nie

      public static <E> Set<E> nie(Set<E> v)
      Returns null if the specified set is empty, else the set.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The set.
      Returns:
      The set, or null if empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.nullIfEmpty(Set)

    • ie

      public static boolean ie(Collection<?> c)
      Returns true if the specified collection is null or empty.
      Parameters:
      c - The collection.
      Returns:
      true if null or empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.isEmpty(Collection)

    • ie

      public static boolean ie(Map<?,?> m)
      Returns true if the specified map is null or empty.
      Parameters:
      m - The map.
      Returns:
      true if null or empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.isEmpty(Map)

    • ine

      public static boolean ine(Collection<?> c)
      Returns true if the specified collection is not null and not empty.
      Parameters:
      c - The collection.
      Returns:
      true if non-empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.isNotEmpty(Collection)

    • ine

      public static boolean ine(Map<?,?> m)
      Returns true if the specified map is not null and not empty.
      Parameters:
      m - The map.
      Returns:
      true if non-empty.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.isNotEmpty(Map)

    • a2

      @SafeVarargs public static <E> E[][] a2(E[]... v)
      Creates a 2-dimensional array from the specified rows.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The rows.
      Returns:
      A 2-dimensional array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.array2d(Object[][])

    • ao

      public static Object[] ao(Object... v)
      Creates an Object[] from the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      v - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new object array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.objectArray(Object...)

    • ll

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> List<T> ll(T... v)
      Creates a mutable LinkedList from the specified elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new linked list.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.linkedList(Object...)

    • hs

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> Set<T> hs(T... v)
      Creates a mutable HashSet from the specified elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new hash set.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.hashSet(Object...)

    • st

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> Set<T> st(T... v)
      Creates a modifiable, insertion-ordered LinkedHashSet from the specified elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new modifiable set.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.set(Object...)

    • ss

      @SafeVarargs public static <E> SortedSet<E> ss(E... v)
      Creates a sorted TreeSet from the specified elements (null values filtered out).
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The elements.
      Returns:
      A new sorted set.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.sortedSet(Object...)

    • u

      public static <T> List<T> u(List<? extends T> v)
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified list.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The list.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(List)

    • u

      public static <K, V> Map<K,V> u(Map<? extends K,? extends V> v)
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified map.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      v - The map.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable map.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(Map)

    • u

      public static <T> Set<T> u(Set<? extends T> v)
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified set.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The set.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable set.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(Set)

    • u

      public static <T> SortedSet<T> u(SortedSet<T> v)
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the specified sorted set.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The sorted set.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable sorted set.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.unmodifiable(SortedSet)

    • len

      public static int len(Object v)
      Returns the length/size of the specified object (array/collection/map/string).
      Parameters:
      v - The object.
      Returns:
      The length, or 0 if null.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.length(Object)

    • cp

      public static <E> List<E> cp(List<E> v)
      Returns a mutable copy of the specified list.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The list to copy.
      Returns:
      A new list containing the same elements.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.copyOf(List)

    • aa

      @SafeVarargs public static <E> List<E> aa(List<E> v, E... e)
      Adds the specified elements to the given list and returns it.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      v - The list to add to.
      e - The elements to add.
      Returns:
      The same list.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.addAll(List,Object...)

    • ints

      public static int[] ints(int... v)
      Creates an int[] from the specified values.
      Parameters:
      v - The values.
      Returns:
      A new int array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.intArray(int...)

    • longs

      public static long[] longs(long... v)
      Creates a long[] from the specified values.
      Parameters:
      v - The values.
      Returns:
      A new long array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.longArray(long...)

      Creates a byte[] from the specified int values.
      Parameters:
      v - The values.
      Returns:
      A new byte array.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.byteArray(int...)

      Parameters:
      v - The values.

      A new char array.

      Parameters:

      A new double array.

      Parameters:

      A new boolean array.

      Parameters:

      A new short array.

    • lb

      public static <E> Lists<E> lb(Class<E> type)
      Creates a new Lists builder for the specified element type.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      type - The element type.
      Returns:
      A new list builder.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.listBuilder(Class)

    • mb

      public static <K, V> Maps<K,V> mb()
      Creates a new Maps builder.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Returns:
      A new map builder.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.mapBuilder()

    • mb

      public static <K, V> Maps<K,V> mb(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType)
      Creates a new Maps builder for the specified key and value types.
      Type Parameters:
      K - The key type.
      V - The value type.
      Parameters:
      keyType - The key type.
      valueType - The value type.
      Returns:
      A new map builder.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.mapBuilder(Class,Class)

    • stb

      public static <E> Sets<E> stb(Class<E> type)
      Creates a new Sets builder for the specified element type.
      Type Parameters:
      E - The element type.
      Parameters:
      type - The element type.
      Returns:
      A new set builder.
      See Also:
      • CollectionUtils.setBuilder(Class)

    • cast

      public static <T> T cast(Class<T> c, Object o)
      Casts the specified object to the specified type (null-safe).
      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      c - The target class.
      o - The object to cast.
      Returns:
      The cast object, or null if the object is null.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.castTo(Class,Object)

    • sa

      public static boolean sa(Method m)
      Makes the specified method accessible.
      Parameters:
      m - The method.
      Returns:
      true if the method was made accessible.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.setAccessible(Method)

    • sa

      public static boolean sa(Field f)
      Makes the specified field accessible.
      Parameters:
      f - The field.
      Returns:
      true if the field was made accessible.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.setAccessible(Field)

    • sa

      public static boolean sa(Constructor<?> c)
      Makes the specified constructor accessible.
      Parameters:
      c - The constructor.
      Returns:
      true if the constructor was made accessible.
      See Also:
      • ClassUtils.setAccessible(Constructor)

    • rex

      public static RuntimeException rex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates a RuntimeException with a formatted message.

    • rex

      public static RuntimeException rex(Throwable t)
      Creates a RuntimeException wrapping a cause.

    • rex

      public static RuntimeException rex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a RuntimeException with a cause and formatted message.

    • iaex

      public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates an IllegalArgumentException with a formatted message.

    • iaex

      public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(Throwable t)
      Creates an IllegalArgumentException wrapping a cause.

    • iaex

      public static IllegalArgumentException iaex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates an IllegalArgumentException with a cause and formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with a formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException wrapping a cause.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with a cause and formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Class<?> c, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with an associated class and formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(ClassInfo c, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with an associated class and formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, Class<?> c, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with a cause, associated class, and formatted message.

    • brex

      public static BeanRuntimeException brex(Throwable t, ClassInfo c, String m, Object... a)
      Creates a BeanRuntimeException with a cause, associated class, and formatted message.

    • ioex

      public static IOException ioex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates an IOException with a formatted message.

    • ioex

      public static IOException ioex(Throwable t)
      Creates an IOException wrapping a cause.

    • ioex

      public static IOException ioex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates an IOException with a cause and formatted message.

    • exex

      public static ExecutableException exex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates an ExecutableException with a formatted message.

    • exex

      public static ExecutableException exex(Throwable t)
      Creates an ExecutableException wrapping a cause.

    • exex

      public static ExecutableException exex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates an ExecutableException with a cause and formatted message.

    • isex

      public static IllegalStateException isex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates an IllegalStateException with a formatted message.

    • uoex

      public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex()
      Creates an UnsupportedOperationException with the message "Not supported."

    • uoex

      public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(String m, Object... a)
      Creates an UnsupportedOperationException with a formatted message.

    • uoex

      public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(Throwable t)
      Creates an UnsupportedOperationException wrapping a cause.

    • uoex

      public static UnsupportedOperationException uoex(Throwable t, String m, Object... a)
      Creates an UnsupportedOperationException with a cause and formatted message.

    • uoroex

      public static UnsupportedOperationException uoroex()
      Creates an UnsupportedOperationException with the message "Object is read only."

    • gst

      public static String gst(Throwable t)
      Returns the stack trace of the specified throwable as a string.
      Parameters:
      t - The throwable.
      Returns:
      The stack trace string.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.getStackTrace(Throwable)

    • cex

      public static <T> T cex(Class<T> c, Throwable t)
      Casts the specified throwable to the specified type, wrapping if necessary.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      c - The target class.
      t - The throwable.
      Returns:
      The cast (or wrapped) throwable.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.castException(Class,Throwable)

    • fc

      public static <T extends Throwable> Optional<T> fc(Throwable e, Class<T> c)
      Searches the causal chain of the specified throwable for a cause of the specified type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The cause type.
      Parameters:
      e - The throwable to search.
      c - The cause class to look for.
      Returns:
      An optional containing the matching cause, if found.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.findCause(Throwable,Class)

    • gtc

      public static <T extends Throwable> T gtc(Class<T> c, Throwable t)
      Returns the first cause of the specified type from the throwable's causal chain.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The cause type.
      Parameters:
      c - The cause class to look for.
      t - The throwable to search.
      Returns:
      The matching cause, or null if not found.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.getThrowableCause(Class,Throwable)

    • lm

      public static String lm(Throwable t)
      Returns the localized message of the specified throwable (null-safe).
      Parameters:
      t - The throwable.
      Returns:
      The localized message, or null if the throwable is null.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.localizedMessage(Throwable)

    • quiet

      public static void quiet(Snippet s)
      Runs the specified snippet, silently swallowing any thrown exception.
      Parameters:
      s - The snippet to run.
      See Also:
      • ThrowableUtils.runQuietly(Snippet)

    • t

      public static <T> boolean t(Predicate<T> p, T v)
      Tests the specified value against the given predicate (null-safe).
      Type Parameters:
      T - The value type.
      Parameters:
      p - The predicate.
      v - The value to test.
      Returns:
      The result of the predicate, or false if the predicate is null.
      See Also:
      • PredicateUtils.test(Predicate,Object)
